# Seven-Day Launch Email and Post Pack

**Asset 5 of 5 — Sell the Client Docs You Already Use**

---

## Purpose

Drive your first buyers in 7 days using 7 email templates and 7 LinkedIn posts.
No large audience required. These are designed to work from zero.

**Time required:** 1–2 hours to customize all 7 posts and emails
**What you need:** Your live Gumroad product URL, your Tally quiz URL

## 7-Day Email Sequence

### EMAIL 1 — Send: Day 1 of launch week

**Subject:** You don't have to build your process from scratch

**Body:**
```
Hey [First Name],

If you've worked with clients for more than a year,
you've already built something valuable.

Not just your writing or your strategy.
Your process.

The way you run discovery. The questions you ask before starting.
The documents you've refined across dozens of projects.

That process — the one clients have paid you to use —
is worth more than one client fee.

Other copywriters and marketers at an earlier stage
would pay $19–$49 for the blank version of what you've built.

This week, I'm sharing how.

[Your Name]

P.S. If you want to see where you're starting from,
take the 2-minute quiz first: {{TALLY_QUIZ_LINK}}
```

---

### EMAIL 2 — Send: Day 2

**Subject:** The document sitting in your Google Drive

**Body:**
```
Hey [First Name],

Most of the people I talk to have the same instinct:

"My stuff isn't good enough to sell."

Here's what's actually true:

The bar isn't perfection. It's usefulness.

A 20-question intake form that stops scope creep is worth $19.
A 2-page brand voice framework that makes client feedback clear is worth $29.
The question isn't whether it's good enough —
it's whether another copywriter or marketer would find it helpful.

If you've used the document with at least 3 clients and it worked,
it's good enough.

Tomorrow: how to find the one to sell first.

[Your Name]

P.S. Your Gumroad listing can be live in 4 days from today.
Get the Starter Kit: {{GUMROAD_LINK}}
```

---

### EMAIL 3 — Send: Day 3

**Subject:** How to choose the right document to sell first

**Body:**
```
Hey [First Name],

Here's the exercise I use to find the first sellable doc:

Step 1: List every document you've sent to more than one client.
Proposals, intake forms, briefs, frameworks, checklists, SOPs.
Just the ones you've actually used — not the ones you planned to use.

Step 2: Score each one:
- Reusability (1–5): how often do you actually reuse the structure?
- Demand (1–5): would another copywriter or marketer want this?
- Specificity (1–5, reversed): how easy is it to remove client-specific details?

Step 3: Add up: Reusability + Demand + (6 minus Specificity). Max score = 15.
Anything scoring 10 or higher is a strong first product.

Step 4: Choose the highest-scoring one you've used most recently.

The Starter Kit includes the full Client Docs Inventory Sheet
with scoring tables and validation questions:
{{GUMROAD_LINK}}

[Your Name]
```

---

### EMAIL 4 — Send: Day 4 (Main offer email)

**Subject:** Build it this afternoon. List it tomorrow.

**Body:**
```
Hey [First Name],

Here's the workflow for turning your document into a paid template:

1. Open the client document you've chosen.
   Remove anything client-specific — names, company details, project specifics.

2. Paste the cleaned-up version into Claude or ChatGPT:
   "Turn this into a reusable template that a [PROFESSION] could use with any client.
   Add instructions for each section. Keep the tone practical. Format with headers."

3. Review and add your voice. Takes 30–60 minutes.

4. Save as Google Doc. Export as PDF. Upload to Gumroad at $19.

That's the process. One afternoon.

The Starter Kit walks through every step with AI prompts
that are already written for copywriters and marketers:
{{GUMROAD_LINK}}

[Your Name]

P.S. Use code TEMPLATE5 at checkout for $5 off. Expires in 48 hours.
```

---

### EMAIL 5 — Send: Day 5

**Subject:** What to write on your Gumroad page

**Body:**
```
Hey [First Name],

The part most people get stuck on isn't creating the template.
It's writing the Gumroad listing.

Here's the simplest structure that converts:

Section 1: Who it's for and what frustration it solves (3–4 lines)
Section 2: What's included — specific items, not vague descriptions
Section 3: "This is for you if..." — 4 situations your buyer recognizes
Section 4: "This is NOT for you if..." — honest and short
Section 5: Price and download format

You don't need a lot of copy. You need specific copy.

"30-question client intake form for freelance copywriters"
converts better than
"comprehensive briefing document for creative professionals."

The Starter Kit includes the Gumroad Sales Page Template —
a fill-in-the-blank structure you can complete in 30 minutes:
{{GUMROAD_LINK}}

[Your Name]
```

---

```
You don't need 5,000 LinkedIn followers to sell your first template.

2. One post per day for 7 days that talks about the problem, not the product
3. The quiz link in your LinkedIn bio and at the end of every post
4. An email sequence that closes for you while you sleep

The buyers are the people who found your content useful enough
to click the quiz and give you their email.

That's a small list — but they already trust you.

50 engaged subscribers beats 5,000 passive followers.

The Starter Kit's 7-Day Launch Email + Post Pack includes
the 7 LinkedIn posts — ready to publish:
{{GUMROAD_LINK}}

[Your Name]
```

## 7 LinkedIn Posts for Launch Week

### POST 1 — Day 1

**Type:** Problem observation (no CTA)

```
Most copywriters charge per project.

A few turn their process into a second income stream.

The difference isn't talent.

It's whether they see their best documents as something to package,
not just use.

The proposal you've refined over 20 clients is worth $19 to the copywriter
who's still building theirs from scratch.

The intake form that stops scope creep is worth $29
to the freelancer who's still getting surprised mid-project.

You already built the product.
You just haven't listed it.
```

---

### POST 2 — Day 2

**Type:** Reframe (challenge a common belief)

```
"My stuff isn't good enough to sell."

I hear this from copywriters with 3 years of client work.

Here's the actual bar:

Would another copywriter find this helpful?

That's it.

A messy 20-question intake form is worth more than no intake form.
A rough messaging framework is worth more than starting from scratch.

Your template doesn't need to be perfect.
It needs to solve a problem someone else has right now.
```

---

### POST 3 — Day 3

**Type:** Practical tip (actionable)

```
How to find out if your client document is worth selling:

Step 1: List every doc you've sent to more than one client
Step 2: Score each one: Reusability + Demand + (6 minus Specificity)
Step 3: Highest score → first product candidate

The threshold: 10 out of 15.

If your document scores 10+:
— You've used it multiple times (it works)
— Others would want it (there's demand)
— You can remove the client-specific parts quickly

That's the criteria. Nothing else.

What document are you scoring right now?
```

---

### POST 4 — Day 4

**Type:** Story / example

```
A freelance copywriter I know had an intake questionnaire
she'd refined across 40+ client projects.

She never thought to sell it.

She listed it on Gumroad at $19.
First sale: within 48 hours.
10 sales by the end of month one.

Not because she had a big following.
Because the document was genuinely useful
and she described the problem it solved clearly.

The product was already built.
She just hadn't packaged it yet.
```

---

### POST 6 — Day 6

**Type:** Objection handling

```
"I don't have enough followers to sell anything."

Here's what I've seen work at a small list size:

1 LinkedIn post per day about the problem
Quiz link in bio and at the end of every post
Email sequence that explains the value over 7 days
A clear Gumroad page that tells buyers exactly what they're getting

The buyers aren't your followers.
They're the people who found your content useful enough
to click the quiz and give you their email.

50 engaged subscribers who know what you do
beats 5,000 passive followers who don't.

The first sale usually happens at under 100 subscribers.
```

---

## How to Schedule Your Posts

**LinkedIn:**
- Post once per day, 7 days in a row
- Best time: 8–10am or 12–1pm on weekdays
- Engage with comments within the first hour of posting
- Do not add hashtags to LinkedIn posts (reduces reach on the platform)
- Do not include the link in the post body — put it in the first comment

**Threads:**
- Post a shorter version of each LinkedIn post (2–4 lines max)
- You can post same-day as LinkedIn or 1 day offset
- Add your quiz link directly in the Threads post (link preview appears)

**Reddit:**
- Do not post or drop links in subreddits
- Use Reddit for research: read r/freelance, r/copywriting, r/marketing
  to understand the language your buyers use
- If you reply to posts, reply helpfully with no links

---

## What to Watch

After Day 3 of posting, check these numbers:

| Metric | Target (30 days) |
|---|---|
| Quiz completions | 50+ |
| Email list growth | 30+ subscribers |
| Gumroad page views | 200+ |
| Sales | 5+ |

If quiz completions are low: the problem isn't the product, it's post reach.
Post more consistently and reply to comments.

If sales are low but quiz completions are high: check your Gumroad page copy.
Run the AI review prompt from Asset 4.
